markdown:
#  Create Custom Submittal Log Statuses 
##  Things to Consider 
  * **Required User Permission** :
    * 'Admin' level permissions on the Company level Admin tool
  * **Additional Information** :
    * Custom submittal log statuses are used to show the state of a submittal (i.e., _Draft_ , _Open_ , _Closed_). Remember that _submittal log status_ is different from _custom submittal responses_ , which are used by the people on the Submittal Workflow who are responsible for reviewing and approving a submittal (i.e., _Approved_ , _Revise and Resubmit_ , _Rejected_ , and so on). For details on customizing responses, see [Manage Custom Submittal Responses](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/submittals-project/tutorials/manage-custom-submittal-responses).


##  Demo 
![Create Custom Submittal Log Status](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/33dbeb931209cdb7c2bf49138460ef18/9d20c70fee3e44ea16229df5320c7dbd/create-custom-submittal-log-status.gif?fm=webp)
##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the company's **Admin** tool.  
This reveals the Company Settings page.
  2. Under "Tool Settings," click **Submittals.**
  3. Under **Custom Submittal Log Statuses** , do the following:
    1. **Name**. Enter a name for the new custom submittal log status in the box provided. For example, _Awaiting Approval_ , _Rejected_ , or _Approved_.
    2. **Status**. Select _Open_ or _Closed_ from the drop-down list. 
##### Example
Submittals can be classified as 'Open' (e.g., "Awaiting Approval"), 'Draft' (e.g., A submittal that has been created, but is not ready to send to the design team for review/approval), and 'Closed' (e.g., "Approved"). You can alternatively select one of your custom statuses from the list.
  4. Click **Create**.   
The system adds your new custom status to the list.   
_Notes_ :
     * Your new custom status becomes available in the Submittals tool on the Create and Edit Submittal page under the **General Information** area in the **Status** drop down list. See [Create a Submittal](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/submittals-project/tutorials/create-a-submittal) and [Edit a Submittal](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/submittals-project/tutorials/edit-a-submittal).
     * If a custom status is applied to a submittal with the Submittals tool, you will NOT be able to delete that status. You are only permitted to delete statuses that are NOT in use.


## See Also
  * [Configure Administrative Settings: Company Admin](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/admin-company/tutorials/configure-administrative-settings-company-admin)
  * [Create Custom Submittal Types](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/admin-company/tutorials/create-custom-submittal-types)
  * [What gets copied over to a new project from a project template?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-gets-copied-over-to-a-new-project-when-applying-a-project-template)


**Submittal Workflow**. The people assigned to complete the [submittal workflow](https://v2.support.procore.com/glossary-of-terms). In Procore, the submittal workflow includes two roles: a [submitter](https://v2.support.procore.com/glossary-of-terms) and the approvers who are responsible for performing/completing the [approval process](https://v2.support.procore.com/glossary-of-terms). Typically, approvers are members of the design team.
title: Create Custom Submittal Log Statuses
description: How to create a custom ﻿﻿submittal log status that provides a different description than the default 'Open' or 'Closed' states.
